query:
back over to issue 409.. currently scoping.  Looks like we gave it bad information... maintain:true is no longer supported.  ALL maintenance is on the background tick in 1 form or another: 1. co_access cleanup — the issue replaces the hard time cutoff   
  with cycle-activity logic. But crt-035 just shipped bidirectional
   promotion. Is the co_access GC logic still needed now that      
  signals get promoted to GRAPH_EDGES, or is it scope creep?     
  2. Entry auto-deprecation — the ROADMAP notes is_adaptive() from
  #445 "for entry auto-deprecation" in the context of #409. But the
   issue body doesn't mention entry auto-deprecation at all. Is
  that in scope or was it a speculative connection?                
  3. maintain=true only vs. background tick — the issue says all GC
   remains opt-in via maintain=true. That's fine for the 152 MB    
  observations problem short-term, but is there appetite to also
  add a background-tick path (or is that deliberately deferred)?   
  4. Eval gate — there's a distribution gate for scoring changes,
  but this is pure GC. Should there be a gate (e.g. verify         
  observations row count drops as expected, no knowledge record
  loss)?

entries.title: Use context_correct to supersede an ADR — never context_deprecate then context_store separately
co_access write-time path has no quarantine guard — correctness relies solely on tick-side JOIN filter
